content: By 2025, the recursive self-improvement loop inherent to autonomous agent self-supervised learning can be reframed to let an agent treat its own curiosity-drive internal reinforcement learning signal as a modular, swappable sub-agent that actively curates which new environment experiences get encoded into long-term memory. This creates a nested agent architecture where the top-level learning agent selects and trains its own curiosity sub-agents to better align exploratory behavior with emergent long-term learning goals, rather than relying on fixed intrinsic reward frameworks like ICM or RND.
